page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the server supports a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores, alongside top-tier large-block read memory bandwidth at 338.15 GB/sec. Single-core performance remains average, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core metrics. Database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong performance, reaching 107.97 million operations per second for Redis.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture delivers high resource density, combining high-capacity local NVMe storage with massive memory allocations. However, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for accelerated deep learning, though it handles CPU-based LLM inference at average speeds. This server is configured for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 200 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth, though single-core metrics remain in the average tier. The server delivers high resource density for memory-intensive applications, though the absence of dedicated GPU hardware limits its suitability for accelerated machine learning tasks. It is configured for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps networking, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and high-bandwidth networking for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1024 GB DDR4 RAM for memory-intensive applications
7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
Suited for large-scale databases, caching, and data processing
